title: Cross-WIN sweep: sign-only / directional-match non-discrimination pattern (Part 2 sibling to axial-symmetry)
target: data/sections.json ["part2"].html — INSERT new sibling subsection "Sign-Only Predictions: A Recurring Non-Discriminating Defense Pattern" between existing #axial-symmetry close and #cosmological-cluster open. New <details id="sign-only-non-discrimination"> block with progressive-disclosure wrapper. Names WIN-059, WIN-060, WIN-066 as the canonical cases; classifies WIN-058 as adjacent (definitional circularity, cross-linked to Section 2.9). Provides three counters that any future sign-only WIN review can invoke without re-derivation.
curmudgeon_review: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-066.c5.json (hole h3 — flagged as out-of-scope for c5 verification, recommended as Section-4 expansion or holistic cross-WIN sweep)
kernel_of_truth: There ARE real physical asymmetries here: the NMP's deceleration in the Siberian sector, the SAA's westward-cell drift, and the NH-vs-SH heat asymmetry are all genuine, well-measured signals worth predicting. The dome correctly identifies them as worth claiming. The failure is at the derivation step: the dome adopts the observed signs from published datasets and counts the match as confirmation, but does not derive the magnitudes from its own parameters. Where the dome DID derive a magnitude (the inverse-cube h=5,733 km flux integral for WIN-066), the answer is wrong by ~250–800×. The kernel that survives: signed observations are real and the dome was right to notice them. The kernel that fails: a sign-match against an alternative model that gets the same sign for unrelated reasons is non-discriminating, and that is what these three WINs collectively share.
summary_for_decider: Single-patch HOLISTIC EXP closing ISS-1926 (WIN-066.c5 hole h3 — cross-WIN systemic issue flagged by curmudgeon as out-of-scope for c5 verification). The c5 review explicitly recommended either (a) a holistic-review item in the curmudgeon tracker for cross-WIN sign-match-non-discrimination, or (b) an analyst Part-2 expansion adding a named sibling subsection. This EXP takes path (b). The new <details id="sign-only-non-discrimination"> block inserts between #axial-symmetry and #cosmological-cluster in part2.html (Self-Contradictions). It names WIN-059, WIN-060, WIN-066 as the canonical cases (NMP-Siberian deceleration, SAA westward drift, NH heat asymmetry), classifies WIN-058 as adjacent (definitional circularity rather than sign-only, cross-linked to Section 2.9), and provides three reusable counters: independent sign-derivation under standard physics, cited-value commits dome to magnitude, sign-only with no derivation = 50/50 prior. Also preempts the strongest defender counter-pivot — "you are using globe mechanisms to refute the dome" — by separating direct empirical observables (continental areas, CERES fluxes, observatory readings) from the geometry under dispute. Operating effect: future sign-only WINs at intake should be re-classified rather than counted; per-WIN detail_verdict_texts can cross-reference this subsection rather than re-derive the three counters. Tier: holistic (cross-WIN scope, new sibling subsection, multi-WIN claims). Curmudgeon should treat as singleton, not batchable.

patches.new_string:
Sign-Only Predictions: A Recurring Non-Discriminating Defense Pattern

Three V51 confirmations (NMP deceleration sector, SAA westward cell shift, NH heat excess) share a structural defect: when the dome's quantitative derivation fails or is absent, a defender retreats to "we predicted the SIGN, not the magnitude." But standard physics independently predicts the same sign through unrelated mechanisms, so a sign-match is non-discriminating between the two models. Each per-WIN defense becomes a coin flip and collapses the confirmation.

Three V51 WINs sit in the same defensive posture once their quantitative content is removed. WIN-059 ("NMP deceleration Siberian") predicts a sector-specific slowdown of the magnetic pole's northward drift; the dome's axially symmetric H(r) = 8,537·exp(−r/8,619) is mechanically incapable of producing sector-specific behaviour, yet the same observed deceleration is derived quantitatively by Livermore et al. (2020) from changing Siberian-versus-Canadian flux-lobe intensities — a globe-MHD mechanism whose rate falls out of core-flow inversion. WIN-060 ("SAA western cell shift") cites the cell's westward drift as confirmation; westward drift has been a published observable since Halley's 1701 declination chart, and Finlay et al. (2016) derives the modern rate (~0.2°/yr) from core-mantle-boundary flow inversions. WIN-066 ("NH heat excess") cites the +0.34 W/m²/decade trend; Loeb et al. (2025) attributes the trend to differential land-mass distribution (~39% NH vs ~19% SH), albedo asymmetry, and aerosol/cloud forcing. In each case, both the dome and the standard model predict the same direction. The standard model also produces the quantitative magnitude; the dome does not.

The defender pivot. Once a dome reviewer notes the missing magnitude — for WIN-059, a 39.9% error against the dome's own NMP-drift Test 6; for WIN-066, the ~250–800× over-prediction from the inverse-cube integral of h = 5,733 km dome geometry — a defender can pivot to: "We never claimed the magnitude. The dome predicted the SIGN, and the sign matches." This collapses the WIN onto its weakest content. The pivot succeeds only if the sign is informative. It is not. A coin flip would land on the predicted sign half the time; standard physics lands on it for documented mechanistic reasons. The Bayesian update from a sign-match is therefore zero or near-zero — the same observation is equally expected under both hypotheses, so it cannot distinguish them.

WIN-058 borderline. WIN-058 ("Unified angular coordinate θ = −lonE") is an adjacent member of this family rather than a directional match per se: its claim is a coordinate identity that follows from the definition of geographic longitude, true for any polar-azimuthal projection regardless of dome physics. We classified that one "Misleading" rather than "Std Model Explains" because it is closer to definitional circularity than to sign-only confirmation; the family resemblance is that both shapes of WIN survive falsification not by being right but by being structurally untestable. Section 2.9 covers the coordinate-loop pattern in depth.

Three counters that close the pivot for any new WIN. The WIN-066 detail_verdict_text now carries the closure paragraph for that specific case. The three counters generalize, and a curmudgeon reviewing any future sign-only WIN should apply them in order. (1) Independent sign-derivation under the standard model: if mainstream physics derives the same sign from unrelated mechanisms (Siberian flux dynamics, CMB flow inversion, land-mass-asymmetry forcing), the sign is non-discriminating regardless of the dome's claim. (2) Cited value commits the dome to magnitude: when the dome's WIN cites a specific number (a drift rate, a westward shift coordinate, a +0.34 W/m²/decade), the WIN is staked on that number — disclaiming the magnitude post-hoc is a retreat from the WIN's own framing, not a clarification of it. (3) Sign-only with no derivation is a 50/50 prior: a model that predicts only sign, with no derivation, has the same falsifiability content as a coin flip — confirmation is information-free. Either the dome predicts a magnitude derived from its parameters (and is wrong by ~250–800× on WIN-066, 39.9% on WIN-059, or has no derivation at all on WIN-060), or it predicts only sign (and any positive value would have confirmed, which is not confirmation in any operational sense).

Anticipated counter-pivot — "you are using globe mechanisms to refute a dome model." A sophisticated defender will note that Counter (1) invokes globe-model mechanisms (Siberian flux lobes, MHD core flow, NH continental albedo) to argue that the sign is non-discriminating, and may protest: "of course the standard model predicts the same sign — you assume the standard model." Two replies. First, the underlying observables in those mechanisms — continental land-mass percentages, observatory readings of the geomagnetic field, CERES top-of-atmosphere energy fluxes — are direct measurements independent of the geometry dispute. The standard model derives the magnitudes from those observables; the dome adopts the numbers. The mechanism is not "imported"; it is the only derivation on the table. Second, this defender move concedes the point: if the dome's case for a WIN depends on rejecting the only mechanistic derivation that fits the observation, the dome must publish its own quantitative derivation from its own parameters. Until it does, the WIN is a sign-match against a model the dome rejects, which is not a confirmation of anything.

Auditor rule going forward. When a new WIN is added to the dome's prediction list and the dome's quantitative derivation either fails or is absent, the WIN should be re-classified rather than counted as a confirmation. If the dome publishes a value derived from disc radius, firmament height, sun altitude, or moon altitude that matches observation, that is a legitimate confirmation candidate. If the dome adopts an observed value and frames the match as sign-only, that is non-discriminating against the standard model and earns "Std Model Explains" or "Misleading" at intake, not "Confirmed." The cross-WIN pattern identified here is what makes per-WIN closure rhetorically expensive; naming it once in this subsection lets the per-WIN detail_verdict_texts cross-reference rather than re-derive.
patches.rationale: Inserts the new <details id="sign-only-non-discrimination"> sibling subsection at the boundary between #axial-symmetry close and #cosmological-cluster open. The old_string is unique in part2.html (verified: 1 occurrence). The new block is self-contained (opens and closes <details> within the inserted region), preserves both flanking sections byte-for-byte, and adds no orphan tags. Insertion order chosen so axial-symmetry (longitude-vs-radial-symmetry paradox) flows into the sibling sign-only-pattern (defender-pivot pattern), then into cosmological-cluster (V51 expansion).
patches.regression_risk: low — pure insertion at a unique boundary in part2.html; no existing prose modified; new section uses canonical progressive-disclosure wrapper; cross-references (#2-9-the-v13-coordinate-system-a-self-referential-loop) verified to exist in part2; DOI links to Livermore 2020, Finlay 2016, Loeb 2025 are all already cited elsewhere in our review; no new external claim introduced that is not already in WIN-059/060/066 detail prose.

Finlay et al. 2016 doi:10.1038/nature21807 — already cited in WIN-060 detail prose

Section 2.9 (id=2-9-the-v13-coordinate-system-a-self-referential-loop) — verified to exist in part2.html

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: "Sign-only predictions are still informative if the prior is 50/50. You are dismissing legitimate confirmations."
anticipated_objections.response: The priors are not 50/50 here. Standard physics independently derives the same sign through unrelated mechanisms (Livermore flux-lobe dynamics for WIN-059, Finlay CMB-flow inversion for WIN-060, Loeb land-mass+aerosol for WIN-066). The Bayesian update is the ratio P(obs|dome) / P(obs|std). When both numerators and denominators are ~1, the update is ~0. The 50/50 prior would apply only against a uniform null hypothesis, not against the actual alternative model on offer.

anticipated_objections.objection: Dome defender: "You are cherry-picking which WINs are sign-only. The dome makes plenty of quantitative predictions too."
anticipated_objections.response: We are not classifying the dome's WHOLE prediction list as sign-only. We are naming the pattern that emerges in WIN-059/060/066 specifically when their quantitative content fails or is absent. WIN-059 has Test 6 at 39.9% error (dome's own monitoring). WIN-060 has no dome-side derivation of the drift rate (we checked — no monitor.py entry). WIN-066 has the ~250–800× inverse-cube over-prediction. The sign-only retreat is the defender's pivot under pressure, not our framing of the original claim. We apply the standard the defender retreats to, not the one we started with.

anticipated_objections.objection: Dome defender: "Counter (1) invokes globe mechanisms — you are using the model you want to disprove as evidence against the dome."
anticipated_objections.response: Two replies. First, the observables those mechanisms rest on — continental land-mass percentages, INTERMAGNET observatory readings, CERES top-of-atmosphere fluxes — are direct empirical measurements independent of the geometry dispute. The standard model derives quantitative magnitudes from those observables; the dome adopts the numbers without derivation. Second, the defender move concedes the point: if the dome needs to reject the only mechanistic derivation on the table, the dome must publish its own. Until then, the sign-match is against a model the dome rejects, which is not confirmation.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: "WIN-058 does not fit the same family — it is a coordinate identity, not a sign-only prediction. Including it weakens the argument."
anticipated_objections.response: Agreed in part — that is why the new section classifies WIN-058 as an adjacent member rather than a canonical case, and cross-links to Section 2.9 (V13 coordinate self-reference) for the proper home of that critique. The family resemblance is structural-untestability (the WIN is true regardless of dome physics), not the specific sign-only shape. Naming it as adjacent rather than central preserves rigor without conceding the relevance of the broader pattern.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: "Strict definition of 'non-discriminating' requires Bayesian formalism — the section asserts 'zero or near-zero update' without quantifying."
anticipated_objections.response: The qualitative claim is sufficient for triage. Quantifying P(sign|dome) and P(sign|std) requires committed priors for each model, which neither side has formalized to that level. The operational claim — that a sign-match without a derivation cannot distinguish two models that both predict the sign — is robust to any reasonable prior. If a future curmudgeon round wants the Bayesian formalism, it can be added as a follow-on patch to this subsection without altering its conclusions.

new_evidence_added: No new external citations. All four DOI/source references (Livermore 2020, Finlay 2016, Loeb 2025, Stephens 2012) are already in the existing WIN-059/060/066 detail prose. The new section consolidates the cross-WIN pattern without introducing fresh empirical claims.
holes_addressed: WIN-066.c5 hole h3 (curmudgeon flagged this as out-of-scope for c5 verification, recommended Section-4 expansion or cross-WIN audit trail). The defender-pivot paragraph in WIN-066.detail_verdict_text was the per-WIN closure; this expansion is the named cross-WIN sweep that subsumes it and provides a reusable rebuttal for any future sign-only WIN.
test_coverage: No numeric/data-blob tests affected. tests/run.sh covers WIN counts, layer percentages, ransomware figures, http_data classifications, etc. — none of which this patch touches. The patch is a pure prose insertion in part2.html. Suggested follow-on: a test that fails if any future WIN with verdict="Std Model Explains" or category="directional-only" lacks a cross-reference to #sign-only-non-discrimination in its detail_verdict_text would operationalize the auditor rule, but that test belongs to a later expansion and is not in scope for ISS-1926.
newsworthy: Naming a recurring dome defense pattern (sign-only retreats as non-discriminating) — modest internal-rigor win but not headline-grade; a non-specialist reader probably wants the per-WIN narratives (WIN-066 over-prediction by 250-800x) rather than the rhetorical-economy improvement.
